pages.title: Ability KeyValues
pages.summary: A DataDriven ability is a collection _KeyValues_. KeyValues are simple, tree-based structures used for storing nested sections containing key/value pairs.
headings: Other keyvalues of the Action Target block

pages.title: AbilityDuration tooltips
pages.summary: **TL;DR:** AbilityDuration is a fairly useless keyvalue because whoever coded it forgot to make an automatic tooltip like with `AbilityDamage`. Use a "duration" AbilityValue and connect it with lua instead.

pages.title: Extending Hero/NPC API with lua modifiers
pages.summary: When creating cool new abilities or game mechanics you often run into issues with the lack of good API. To fix those issues you might want to expand it.

pages.title: Sending Server values to the Client in a modifier.
pages.summary: Modifier scripts are run on both the server, and every client in the game. A lot of the [Lua API](https://moddota.com/api/#!/vscripts) is server-side functions that the client cannot use.

pages.title: The importance of AbilityValues values
pages.summary: _To specify numeric values, you can put in a number or you can use `%name` formatting to grab values out of the "AbilityValues" block of the ability. The advantage to using the `%name` syntax is that the value can change as the ability levels up and the numeric value can be formatted into tooltips._

pages.title: MDL to VMDL Conversion
pages.summary: This guide will go through the process of making a **`.vmdl`** file (Source 2 Model) from a **`.mdl`** file and other model-related files from Source 1. The Wyvern Hatchling courier will be used as example, but the same process applies to heroes, buildings and any Valve Dota model.
headings: Step 1. Acquire the **model** and **material** files from the main game VPK (`pak01_dir.vpk`) using GCFScape.
Step 2. Use Crowbar to decompile the model files into meshes (`.smd`)
Step 3. Use VTFEdit to generate color TGAs out of the VTF files acquired in step 1.
Step 4. Move the generated files to your addons **/content/** folder.
Step 5. Generate a `.vmat` file from the TGA files.
Step 6. Make a New VMDL from the Mesh file (`.smd`)
Step 7. Add a Material Remap for the missing textures, using the .vmat file from step 5.
Step 8. Add the Animations on the `_anims` folder and make Activities for them.

pages.title: Bundling scripts with webpack
pages.summary: Working on a large codebase there are two ways to organize your code. The first is just keeping all logic in a single `.js` file, which quickly becomes hard to change and comprehend. The second approach is splitting code by functionality, creating multiple `.js` files and including all of them in the layout file.

pages.title: Introduction to Panorama UI with TypeScript
pages.summary: [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) is a language created by and for people that were unhappy with Javascript and all of its quirks and flaws. TypeScript is a language with its own syntax (although similar to Javascript) that compiles to Javascript in a way that avoids a lot of Javascript's issues.

pages.title: Typescript Introduction
